mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing present values of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based collapse in consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and depressing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power with IC below 0.01 outside early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers become prohibitive, compressing profit margins and triggering short-term equity market declines.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises confirms structural labor market weakness, reducing aggregate demand and causing sustained equity underperformance.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no statistical edge over single-series models, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product directly expand corporate revenue bases and earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below the 100 baseline during a recessionary contraction signals severe output shocks and supply chain disruptions, prompting immediate risk-off equity positioning.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally when INDPRO falls below 100 during recessions, or the trigger fails to separate winning and losing periods with p greater than 0.05.
